mccaul-michael.text: Michael Mccaul (Republican) is the current officeholder running for U.S. Representative, TX-10.
As of 2026-07-04, FEC records show Michael Mccaul had raised $271,697 and spent $400,946 for this campaign.

On 7-Jan-2025, Michael Mccaul voted Yea on H R 29. A Yea vote required federal detention of unauthorized immigrants charged with theft, burglary, larceny, or assaulting a law enforcement officer, and let state attorneys general sue the federal government over certain immigration enforcement decisions.

eckhardt-sarah-tx10.text: Sarah Eckhardt (Democratic) is a candidate for U.S. Representative, TX-10.
As of 2026-07-04, FEC records show Sarah Eckhardt had raised $113,353 and spent $110,496 for this campaign.
No public voting record in our set for Sarah Eckhardt (not currently a member of Congress).

base.summary: This is the U.S. House race for TX-10. In the most recent election (2024-11-05), Michael McCaul (REP) won by about 29.59 points out of 347818 votes cast. The bullets below summarize each current candidate's public finance and voting-record data where available.
caveats: This summary only includes facts found in our sourced dataset (FEC finance filings and U.S. House Clerk roll-call votes). It omits anything we could not verify from those sources.
No public data in our set on any candidate's policy positions for this race (the positions field was empty for every candidate as of this build).
No public data in our set on Michael Mccaul's policy positions.
No public data in our set on Sarah Eckhardt's policy positions.
